originalTitle: US launches further strikes on Iran; Houthis threaten Saudi shipping blockade – Middle East crisis live
summary: US says latest attack aimed at further degrading Iranian military capabilities; Yemen militant group threatens traffic moving through Bab el-Mandeb strait
content: The latest US-Iran hostilities are likely ⁠to cast a shadow over a diplomatic gathering this week in south-east Asia – a region that relies heavily on Middle East oil and gas and was severely hit by the global supply and price shocks the war triggered. The Middle East crisis is expected to feature prominently in the talks of foreign ministers from the 11-member Association of Southeast Asian Nations ⁠in Manila, said a spokesperson for host the Philippines. “We’re looking at several occasions where they can raise this very, very critical topic,” Dominic Xavier Imperial said, quoted by Reuters. More now on Iran firing missiles at a US base in Kuwait: the army was targeting US Himars missile systems, state news agency Irna said. It quoted Iranian army officials as saying that Himars – or high mobility artillery rocket system – has “the ability to move quickly against ground targets, which, when targeted, causes damage to the offensive and defensive layers and reduces the enemy’s missile power”. Camp Arifjan – south of Kuwait City – houses US military elements including the air force, navy, marines and coast guard. Here’s more on the US military’s 10th consecutive round of strikes on Iran to force the reopening of the strait of Hormuz. William Christou reports that earlier on Monday, Iran’s president said the country was in a “full-scale war” after the US expanded its aerial campaign and Tehran struck Washington’s Gulf allies, even as diplomats said talks to de-escalate were continuing. Iranian media reported strikes around Tabriz, believed to hold underground missile bases, as well as Bushehr, home to the country’s only operational civilian nuclear power plant. The US military said it had struck “military command centres, air defence and coastal surveillance sites, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites and communications networks”. Iran launched attacks against Bahrain – which hosts the US navy’s fifth fleet – and Kuwait, in addition to striking tankers in the strait of Hormuz. Bahrain said its air traffic systems were damaged by the strikes but traffic was not affected. The Iran-allied Houthis in Yemen also announced a blockade of Saudi ports. “The reality is that today the Islamic Republic of Iran is engaged in a full-scale war,” the Iranian president, Masoud Pezeshkian, said on Monday, warning it might have to endure more strikes. US president Donald Trump signalled an escalation in tactics, saying Iran ⁠would “pay” for killing US soldiers “many times over” after multiple service members ‌were killed in action over the weekend. Iran’s army is saying it has targeted US missile systems at Kuwait’s Arifjan base with surface-to-air missiles, according to Iranian state television. The Kuwait army said about an hour ago that its air defences were responding to Iranian missile and drone attacks as Tehran continues striking at US-allied countries around the Gulf region. Democrats have seized on the deaths of three US troops killed in Iranian strikes to urge Donald Trump to urgently reverse his resumption of the war with Iran amid widespread anxiety over climbing casualties. Even as US forces launched a 10th consecutive wave of attacks against Iranian targets, concern over the deaths of American service personnel threatened to complicate moves to escalate the conflict, which has reignited after he last week walked away from a memorandum of understanding agreed with Iran in June. The Pentagon released the names of two soldiers killed in a strike on Muwaffaq Salti air base in Jordan on Friday. They were First Lieutenant Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Private Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas. A third member of the armed forces was killed in northern Iraq on Saturday in what US Central Command called a “controlled detonation” of unexploded ordnance. The remains of a possible fourth dead service member were found over the weekend in Jordan. Chris Murphy, a Democratic senator for Connecticut, accused Trump of having “frighteningly lost control of this war”. A British maritime agency says it has received multiple reports that a tanker is saying it has been struck by an unknown projectile in the strait of Hormuz. It occurred eight nautical miles north-east of Limah, Oman, and authorities are investigating, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency says in a report posted on X. More on this as it comes to light. Kuwait’s air defences are responding to hostile missile and drone attacks launched by Iran, the Kuwaiti army said on Monday. It comes after the US military earlier announced a tenth consecutive night of strikes on Iran. Tehran has been responding with attacks on its Gulf neighbours that host US forces, including Kuwait. Iranian media reported explosions in parts of the country including the port city of Bandar Abbas, after US Central Command announced a new wave of strikes on Iran for the tenth night in a row. The Mehr news agency also reported air defence systems being activated near the Bushehr nuclear power plant. “The strikes are designed to further degrade Iranian military capabilities used to attack commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz,” Centcom said. It came hours after Donald Trump said Iran would pay for killing US soldiers “many times over” after multiple service members ‌were killed in ⁠action over the weekend. The US military on Monday identified two soldiers killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks. An examination of remains is underway to determine whether they belong to a third service member who was reporting missing in that attack. And in Iraq, another US service member died after “a controlled detonation” of an Iranian drone. It brings the total number of US military deaths in the war with Iran, which the US and Israel started in late February, to 17. chineseTitle: 美国对伊朗发动进一步打击；胡塞武装威胁沙特航运封锁——中东危机直播
chineseSummary: 美国称最新的袭击旨在进一步削弱伊朗的军事能力；也门武装组织威胁通过曼德海峡的交通
chineseContent: 最新的美伊敌对行动可能会给本周在东南亚举行的外交聚会蒙上阴影。东南亚是一个严重依赖中东石油和天然气的地区，并受到战争引发的全球供应和价格冲击的严重打击。东道主菲律宾发言人表示，预计中东危机将成为东南亚国家联盟 11 成员国外长在马尼拉举行的会谈中的重要议题。路透社援引多米尼克泽维尔帝国的话说：“我们正在考虑几个场合，让他们提出这个非常非常关键的话题。”国家通讯社伊朗通讯社称，现在有更多关于伊朗向科威特美国基地发射导弹的消息：军队瞄准的是美国的希马尔斯导弹系统。报道援引伊朗军方官员的话说，希玛斯（Himars）或高机动火炮火箭系统具有“对地面目标快速移动的能力，一旦瞄准目标，就会对进攻和防御层造成损害，并削弱敌人的导弹威力”。阿里夫詹营地位于科威特城以南，驻扎着美军，包括空军、海军、海军陆战队和海岸警卫队。以下是有关美军连续第十轮对伊朗发动袭击、迫使霍尔木兹海峡重新开放的更多信息。威廉·克里斯图报道称，周一早些时候，伊朗总统表示，在美国扩大空袭行动、德黑兰袭击华盛顿的海湾盟友后，伊朗正处于一场“全面战争”，尽管外交官们表示，缓和局势的谈判仍在继续。伊朗媒体报道了大不里士（据信拥有地下导弹基地）以及布什尔（该国唯一运营的民用核电站所在地）周围的袭击事件。美国军方表示，袭击了“军事指挥中心、防空和沿海监视站点、海上军事基地”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
